_doc: Gate-writer census ratchet (eng plan S2-PR4 / errata C(iii)). CI fails if the AST census over renquant-pipeline/src exceeds max_buy_blocked_writers. Retirement PRs TIGHTEN this number; it never grows WITHOUT explicit justification recorded here for a genuinely new, structurally-necessary choke point (see the 2026-07-11 entry below). Authority: renquant_orchestrator.engineering_census.

breakdown_at_last_update.kernel/pipeline/task_data_availability.p: 1 designated choke point (DataAvailabilityGateTask.enforce_buy_block, pipeline #187, 2026-07-11) — runs strictly AFTER the sell/exit pass and BEFORE the buy-candidate scan, a pipeline phase none of the other 3 choke points occupy (they all run earlier in InferencePipeline). This ordering is the entire point of #187's P1 safety fix (a data-availability block must never suppress a risk-reducing sell/exit) and cannot be folded into an existing choke point without breaking that guarantee. Dual-writes the GateRegistry (pipeline #189) like the other three.

_floor_doc: 4 = the designated job-boundary choke points (one per pipeline phase with gates). errata C(iii) COMPLETE for the pipeline repo as of #128 for the original 3; task_data_availability.py is a 4th, added 2026-07-11 for a genuinely new pipeline phase (post-sell/pre-buy-scan input-availability gating), not a regression of the migration. Every gate submits to the GateRegistry; only the aggregate-application (and now phase-boundary) points write buy_blocked.
